what_it_does: Implements the Studio welcome screen: a time-aware Vi greeting, an open-ended prompt composer with suggestion chips, a grid of recent projects (active project featured, other projects, and a 'new' card), phase-journey indicators, and footer actions, all wired to callback props for routing.
observations: React hooks are aliased with a 'W' suffix (useStateW, useEffectW, useRefW)
Components are attached to window (WelcomeScreen) and rely on window.MS_DATA for projects and phase metadata
A greeting object is computed once via useRef and reused for the session
The active project is rendered as a large featured card with a ViWhisper note
Other projects and a 'Start something new' card are rendered in a side column
A Composer component accepts vague prompts and chips, forwarding them to onAskVi
The Vi orb is clickable and triggers onOpenLauncher
Footer contains version text and a Settings button that calls onOpenProject('settings')
PhaseStrip and PhaseStripFeatured render progress using window.MS_DATA.PHASES
standouts: Vi persona embedded throughout the UI via greeting, whisper, orb, and chips
Featured active project card with contextual AI-generated note
Phase journey visualized both as a rail and as a dot strip
conventions: Functional React components with props destructuring
Global window namespace for shared data and component registration
Callback props for navigation/routing (onOpenProject, onStartWizard, onAskVi, onOpenLauncher)
concerns: Settings is opened via onOpenProject('settings'), which may conflate project IDs with app routes
activeProject is assumed to exist without a guard
submit() is defined but never called inside the file; Composer uses onAskVi directly
greetingRef is computed only at mount, so it will not update if the hour changes during the session
Heavy dependency on window.MS_DATA without visible fallback
notes: A concrete, persona-heavy welcome screen that surfaces recent work and captures vague intent through a composer, but hardcodes the user name and leans on global window data.
questions: Where are Composer, ViMark, ViMemoryButton, Icon, and Btn defined?
Is 'Sarah' a placeholder or tied to a specific user identity source?
What is the semantics of the data-comment-anchor attribute?
How does onOpenProject('settings') resolve to a settings screen rather than a project?
What is the exact shape of window.MS_DATA.PHASES and project objects?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation closely; it additionally exposes a launcher entry point via the Vi orb, which is not mentioned in the header comment.
intention_signals: To make the Studio feel like a personal assistant that greets the user, immediately surfaces what they were doing, and lowers the barrier to starting or resuming work by accepting loose natural-language prompts.
novelty: The persistent ViWhisper voice inside project cards and the time-of-day personalized greeting blend an AI persona directly into the project browsing surface rather than isolating it in a chat panel.
contribution: A ready-to-use welcome screen composition that wires together greeting, intent capture, project continuity, and Vi presence, plus reusable phase-indicator components and a Vi-branded inline note component.
summary_for_embedding: welcome.jsx renders the Studio welcome screen with a time-aware Vi greeting, an open prompt composer with chips, a featured active project plus recent projects grid, phase-journey indicators, and routing callbacks.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a low-friction, assistant-led entry point that greets the user, infers intent from vague input, and surfaces ongoing projects for quick continuation.

symbols.does: A local handler intended to forward a non-empty prompt to onAskVi.
symbols.description: Defined as a closure inside WelcomeScreen, submit trims the prompt and calls onAskVi(prompt), but it is not invoked anywhere in this file; the Composer instead calls onAskVi directly on submit.

symbols.does: Renders a compact phase progress rail and label for a project.
symbols.description: Reads phase metadata from window.MS_DATA, computes the current phase index, draws a filled rail proportional to the phase position, and shows the phase icon, label, and rounded progress percentage.

symbols.does: Renders a dot-based phase journey indicator for the featured project card.
symbols.description: Maps over window.MS_DATA.PHASES to render done/active/inactive dots, pulses the active dot, and displays the current phase label plus progress percentage as a legend.

symbols.does: Renders a small Vi-branded inline note or comment.
symbols.description: Wraps children text in a vi-whisper container with a ViMark badge, used inside the featured project card to present a contextual assistant message.

symbols.does: Returns time-of-day salutation, eyebrow label, and greeting line.
symbols.description: Uses the current hour to select one of five greeting variants with an Australian-locale weekday, returning an object consumed by the welcome header.
